rationale: Both candidates independently surface the latent gap (hunyuan_image3_transformer.py:2238 still calling removed get_cache_scale, unswept by this PR), satisfying gap_hit for both. Y goes further by actually cross-checking the real upstream vLLM source (AutoWeightsLoader, fp8.py) to substantiate that the delegated-loader design is sound, and lands a sharper, more on-target critique of the new test (it never drives the raw name through the outer AutoWeightsLoader, so it wouldn't catch a regression in the actual mapping behavior) — closely paralleling the ground-truth reviewer's 'test uses a fake param, doesn't confirm mapped names resolve on a real checkpoint' concern. Y's one weak spot is a specific, likely-fabricated claim that main now references '(see #4810)' for the fix. X's blocker/'FOLLOW-UP REQUIRED' framing overstates severity relative to how it actually played out (merged as non-blocking), and pads findings with lower-value nits (unrelated pre-existing double-prefix code, v_scale/q_scale coverage) that dilute precision. Both are concretely actionable with file/line citations.
